NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Blessed is he who considers the poor; The LORD will deliver him in time of trouble.
NKJV.2: The LORD will preserve him and keep him alive, And he will be blessed on the earth; You will not deliver him to the will of his enemies.
NKJV.3: The LORD will strengthen him on his bed of illness; You will sustain him on his sickbed.
NKJV.4: I said, “LORD, be merciful to me;  Heal my soul, for I have sinned against You.”
NKJV.5: My enemies speak evil of me: “When will he die, and his name perish?”
NKJV.6: And if he comes to see me, he speaks lies; His heart gathers iniquity to itself; When he goes out, he tells it.
NKJV.7: All who hate me whisper together against me; Against me they devise my hurt.
NKJV.8: “An evil disease,” they say, “clings to him. And now that he lies down, he will rise up no more.”
NKJV.9: Even my own familiar friend in whom I trusted, Who ate my bread, Has lifted up his heel against me.
NKJV.10: But You, O LORD, be merciful to me, and raise me up, That I may repay them.
NKJV.11: By this I know that You are well pleased with me, Because my enemy does not triumph over me.
NKJV.12: As for me, You uphold me in my integrity, And set me before Your face forever.
NKJV.13: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel From everlasting to everlasting! Amen and Amen.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Blessed<S>835</S> is he that considereth<S>7919</S> the poor<S>1800</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> will deliver<S>4422</S> him in time<S>3117</S> of trouble<S>7451</S>. <sup>the poor: or, the weak, or, sick</sup> <sup>in time: Heb. in the day of evil</sup>
KJV.2: The LORD<S>3068</S> will preserve<S>8104</S> him, and keep him alive<S>2421</S>; and he shall be blessed<S>833</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>: and thou wilt not deliver<S>5414</S> him unto the will<S>5315</S> of his enemies<S>341</S>. <sup>thou: or, do not thou deliver</sup>
KJV.3: The LORD<S>3068</S> will strengthen<S>5582</S> him upon the bed<S>6210</S> of languishing<S>1741</S>: thou wilt make<S>2015</S> all his bed<S>4904</S> in his sickness<S>2483</S>. <sup>make: Heb. turn</sup>
KJV.4: I said<S>559</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>, be merciful<S>2603</S> unto me: heal<S>7495</S> my soul<S>5315</S>; for I have sinned<S>2398</S> against thee.
KJV.5: Mine enemies<S>341</S> speak<S>559</S> evil<S>7451</S> of me, When shall he die<S>4191</S>, and his name<S>8034</S> perish<S>6</S>?
KJV.6: And if he come<S>935</S> to see<S>7200</S> me, he speaketh<S>1696</S> vanity<S>7723</S>: his heart<S>3820</S> gathereth<S>6908</S> iniquity<S>205</S> to itself; when he goeth<S>3318</S> abroad<S>2351</S>, he telleth<S>1696</S> it.
KJV.7: All that hate<S>8130</S> me whisper<S>3907</S> together<S>3162</S> against me: against me do they devise<S>2803</S> my hurt<S>7451</S>. <sup>my: Heb. evil to me</sup>
KJV.8: An evil<S>1100</S> disease<S>1697</S>, say they, cleaveth fast<S>3332</S> unto him: and now that he lieth<S>7901</S> he shall rise up<S>6965</S> no more<S>3254</S>. <sup>An: Heb. A thing of Belial</sup>
KJV.9: Yea, mine own familiar<S>7965</S> friend<S>376</S>, in whom I trusted<S>982</S>, which did eat<S>398</S> of my bread<S>3899</S>, hath lifted up<S>1431</S> his heel<S>6119</S> against me. <sup>mine: Heb. the man of my peace</sup> <sup>lifted: Heb. magnified</sup>
KJV.10: But thou, O LORD<S>3068</S>, be merciful<S>2603</S> unto me, and raise me up<S>6965</S>, that I may requite<S>7999</S> them.
KJV.11: By this I know<S>3045</S> that thou favourest<S>2654</S> me, because mine enemy<S>341</S> doth not triumph<S>7321</S> over me.
KJV.12: And as for me, thou upholdest<S>8551</S> me in mine integrity<S>8537</S>, and settest<S>5324</S> me before thy face<S>6440</S> for ever<S>5769</S>.
KJV.13: Blessed<S>1288</S> be the LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> from everlasting<S>5769</S>, and to everlasting<S>5769</S>. Amen<S>543</S>, and Amen<S>543</S>.

ESV.1: Blessed is the one who considers the poor!  In the day of trouble the LORD delivers him;
ESV.2: the LORD protects him and keeps him alive; he is called blessed in the land; you do not give him up to the will of his enemies.
ESV.3: The LORD sustains him on his sickbed; in his illness you restore him to full health.
ESV.4: As for me, I said, “O LORD, be gracious to me; heal me,  for I have sinned against you!”
ESV.5: My enemies say of me in malice, “When will he die, and his name perish?”
ESV.6: And when one comes to see me, he utters empty words, while his heart gathers iniquity; when he goes out, he tells it abroad.
ESV.7: All who hate me whisper together about me; they imagine the worst for me.
ESV.8: They say, “A deadly thing is poured out  on him; he will not rise again from where he lies.”
ESV.9: Even my close friend in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted his heel against me.
ESV.10: But you, O LORD, be gracious to me, and raise me up, that I may repay them!
ESV.11: By this I know that you delight in me: my enemy will not shout in triumph over me.
ESV.12: But you have upheld me because of my integrity, and set me in your presence forever.
ESV.13: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! Amen and Amen.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. How blessed is he who considers the helpless; The LORD will deliver him in a day of trouble.
NASB.2: The LORD will protect him and keep him alive, And he shall be called blessed upon the earth; And do not give him over to the desire of his enemies.
NASB.3: The LORD will sustain him upon his sickbed; In his illness, You restore him to health.
NASB.4: As for me, I said, 'O LORD, be gracious to me; Heal my soul, for I have sinned against You.'
NASB.5: My enemies speak evil against me, 'When will he die, and his name perish?'
NASB.6: And when he comes to see [me], he speaks falsehood; His heart gathers wickedness to itself; When he goes outside, he tells it.
NASB.7: All who hate me whisper together against me; Against me they devise my hurt, [saying],
NASB.8: 'A wicked thing is poured out upon him, That when he lies down, he will not rise up again.'
NASB.9: Even my close friend in whom I trusted, Who ate my bread, Has lifted up his heel against me.
NASB.10: But You, O LORD, be gracious to me and raise me up, That I may repay them.
NASB.11: By this I know that You are pleased with me, Because my enemy does not shout in triumph over me.
NASB.12: As for me, You uphold me in my integrity, And You set me in Your presence forever.
NASB.13: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, From everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.

NLT.1:
Oh, the joys of those who are kind to the poor!
The LORD rescues them when they are in trouble.
NLT.2:
The LORD protects them
and keeps them alive.
He gives them prosperity in the land
and rescues them from their enemies.
NLT.3:
The LORD nurses them when they are sick
and restores them to health.
NLT.4:
“O LORD,” I prayed, “have mercy on me.
Heal me, for I have sinned against you.”
NLT.5:
But my enemies say nothing but evil about me.
“How soon will he die and be forgotten?” they ask.
NLT.6:
They visit me as if they were my friends,
but all the while they gather gossip,
and when they leave, they spread it everywhere.
NLT.7:
All who hate me whisper about me,
imagining the worst.
NLT.8:
“He has some fatal disease,” they say.
“He will never get out of that bed!”
NLT.9:
Even my best friend, the one I trusted completely,
the one who shared my food, has turned against me.
NLT.10:
LORD, have mercy on me.
Make me well again, so I can pay them back!
NLT.11:
I know you are pleased with me,
for you have not let my enemies triumph over me.
NLT.12:
You have preserved my life because I am innocent;
you have brought me into your presence forever.
NLT.13:
Praise the LORD, the God of Israel,
who lives from everlasting to everlasting.
Amen and amen!

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Blessed is he who considers the poor. Yahweh will deliver him in the day of evil.
WEB.2: Yahweh will preserve him, and keep him alive. He shall be blessed on the earth, and he will not surrender him to the will of his enemies.
WEB.3: Yahweh will sustain him on his sickbed, and restore him from his bed of illness.
WEB.4: I said, “Yahweh, have mercy on me! Heal me, for I have sinned against you.”
WEB.5: My enemies speak evil against me: “When will he die, and his name perish?”
WEB.6: If he comes to see me, he speaks falsehood. His heart gathers iniquity to itself. When he goes abroad, he tells it.
WEB.7: All who hate me whisper together against me. They imagine the worst for me.
WEB.8: “An evil disease”, they say, “has afflicted him. Now that he lies he shall rise up no more.”
WEB.9: Yes, my own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, who ate bread with me, has lifted up his heel against me.
WEB.10: But you, Yahweh, have mercy on me, and raise me up, that I may repay them.
WEB.11: By this I know that you delight in me, because my enemy doesn’t triumph over me.
WEB.12: As for me, you uphold me in my integrity, and set me in your presence forever.
WEB.13: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting! Amen and amen.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] Happy is one who is considerate of the poor;
the LORD will save him in a day of adversity. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
The LORD will keep him and preserve him;
he will be blessed in the land. ⓜ
You will not give him over to the desire of his enemies. ⓝ
CSB17.3:
The LORD will sustain him on his sickbed;
you will heal him on the bed where he lies. ⓐ
CSB17.4:
I said, “LORD, be gracious to me;
heal me, for I have sinned against you.” ⓑ
CSB17.5:
My enemies speak maliciously about me:
“When will he die and be forgotten?” ⓒ
CSB17.6:
When one of them comes to visit, he speaks deceitfully;
he stores up evil in his heart;
he goes out and talks. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
All who hate me whisper together about me;
they plan to harm me. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
“Something awful has overwhelmed him, [1]
and he won’t rise again from where he lies!” ⓕ
CSB17.9:
Even my friend [2] in whom I trusted,
one who ate my bread,
has raised his heel against me. ⓖ
CSB17.10:
But you, LORD, be gracious to me and raise me up;
then I will repay them. ⓗ
CSB17.11:
By this I know that you delight in me: ⓘ
my enemy does not shout in triumph over me. ⓙ
CSB17.12:
You supported me because of my integrity ⓚ
and set me in your presence forever. ⓛ
CSB17.13:
Blessed be the LORD God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting.
Amen and amen. ⓜ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Blessed [by God's grace and compassion] is he who considers the helpless;
The LORD will save him in the day of trouble.
AMP.2:
The LORD will protect him and keep him alive;
And he will be called blessed in the land;
You do not hand him over to the desire of his enemies.
AMP.3:
The LORD will sustain and strengthen him on his sickbed;
In his illness, You will restore him to health.
AMP.4:
As for me, I said, "O LORD, be gracious to me;
Heal my soul, for I have sinned against You."
AMP.5:
My enemies speak evil of me, saying,
"When will he die and his name perish?"
AMP.6:
And when one comes to see me, he speaks empty words,
While his heart gathers malicious gossip [against me];
When he goes away, he tells it [everywhere].
AMP.7:
All who hate me whisper together about me;
Against me they devise my hurt [imagining the worst for me], saying,
AMP.8:
"A wicked thing is poured out upon him and holds him;
And when he lies down, he will not rise up again."
AMP.9:
Even my own close friend in whom I trusted,
Who ate my bread,
Has lifted up his heel against me [betraying me].
AMP.10:
But You, O LORD, be gracious to me and restore me [to health],
So that I may repay them.
AMP.11:
By this I know that You favor and delight in me,
Because my enemy does not shout in triumph over me.
AMP.12:
As for me, You uphold me in my integrity,
And You set me in Your presence forever.
AMP.13:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
From everlasting to everlasting [from this age to the next, and forever].
Amen and Amen (so be it).

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Dignify those who are down on their luck;
you'll feel good—that's what God does.
MSG.2:
God looks after us all,
makes us robust with life—
Lucky to be in the land,
we're free from enemy worries.
MSG.3:
Whenever we're sick and in bed,
God becomes our nurse,
nurses us back to health.
MSG.4:
I said, "God, be gracious!
Put me together again—
my sins have torn me to pieces."
MSG.5:
My enemies are wishing the worst for me;
they make bets on what day I will die.
MSG.6:
If someone comes to see me,
he mouths empty platitudes,
All the while gathering gossip about me
to entertain the street-corner crowd.
MSG.7:
These "friends" who hate me
whisper slanders all over town.
They form committees
to plan misery for me.
MSG.8:
The rumor goes out, "He's got some dirty,
deadly disease. The doctors
have given up on him."
MSG.9:
Even my best friend, the one I always told everything
—he ate meals at my house all the time!—
has bitten my hand.
MSG.10:
God, give grace, get me up on my feet.
I'll show them a thing or two.
MSG.11:
Meanwhile, I'm sure you're on my side—
no victory shouts yet from the enemy camp!
MSG.12:
You know me inside and out, you hold me together,
you never fail to stand me tall in your presence
so I can look you in the eye.
MSG.13:
Blessed is God, Israel's God,
always, always, always.
Yes. Yes. Yes.

NIV.1:
Psalm 41
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
Blessed is he who has regard for the weak;
the Lord delivers him in times of trouble.
NIV.2:
The Lord will protect him and preserve his life;
he will bless him in the land
and not surrender him to the desire of his foes.
NIV.3:
The Lord will sustain him on his sickbed
and restore him from his bed of illness.
NIV.4:
I said, “O Lord, have mercy on me;
heal me, for I have sinned against you.”
NIV.5:
My enemies say of me in malice,
“When will he die and his name perish?”
NIV.6:
Whenever one comes to see me,
he speaks falsely, while his heart gathers slander;
then he goes out and spreads it abroad.
NIV.7:
All my enemies whisper together against me;
they imagine the worst for me, saying,
NIV.8:
“A vile disease has beset him;
he will never get up from the place where he lies.”
NIV.9:
Even my close friend, whom I trusted,
he who shared my bread,
has lifted up his heel against me.
NIV.10:
But you, O Lord, have mercy on me;
raise me up, that I may repay them.
NIV.11:
I know that you are pleased with me,
for my enemy does not triumph over me.
NIV.12:
In my integrity you uphold me
and set me in your presence forever.
NIV.13:
Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting.
Amen and Amen.

NRSVCE.1:
Happy are those who consider the poor;
the LORD delivers them in the day of trouble.
NRSVCE.2:
The LORD protects them and keeps them alive;
they are called happy in the land.
You do not give them up to the will of their enemies.
NRSVCE.3:
The LORD sustains them on their sickbed;
in their illness you heal all their infirmities.
NRSVCE.4:
As for me, I said, “O LORD, be gracious to me;
heal me, for I have sinned against you.”
NRSVCE.5:
My enemies wonder in malice
when I will die, and my name perish.
NRSVCE.6:
And when they come to see me, they utter empty words,
while their hearts gather mischief;
when they go out, they tell it abroad.
NRSVCE.7:
All who hate me whisper together about me;
they imagine the worst for me.
NRSVCE.8:
They think that a deadly thing has fastened on me,
that I will not rise again from where I lie.
NRSVCE.9:
Even my bosom friend in whom I trusted,
who ate of my bread, has lifted the heel against me.
NRSVCE.10:
But you, O LORD, be gracious to me,
and raise me up, that I may repay them.
NRSVCE.11:
By this I know that you are pleased with me;
because my enemy has not triumphed over me.
NRSVCE.12:
But you have upheld me because of my integrity,
and set me in your presence forever.
NRSVCE.13:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.

NET.1: How blessed is the one who treats the poor properly! When trouble comes, the LORD delivers him.
NET.2: May the LORD protect him and save his life! May he be blessed in the land! Do not turn him over to his enemies!
NET.3: The LORD supports him on his sickbed; you completely heal him from his illness.
NET.4: As for me, I said: “O LORD, have mercy on me! Heal me, for I have sinned against you!
NET.5: My enemies ask this cruel question about me, ‘When will he finally die and be forgotten?’
NET.6: When someone comes to visit, he pretends to be friendly; he thinks of ways to defame me, and when he leaves he slanders me.
NET.7: All who hate me whisper insults about me to one another; they plan ways to harm me.
NET.8: They say, ‘An awful disease overwhelms him, and now that he is bed-ridden he will never recover.’
NET.9: Even my close friend whom I trusted, he who shared meals with me, has turned against me.
NET.10: As for you, O LORD, have mercy on me and raise me up, so I can pay them back!”
NET.11: By this I know that you are pleased with me, for my enemy does not triumph over me.
NET.12: As for me, you uphold me because of my integrity; you allow me permanent access to your presence.
NET.13: The LORD God of Israel deserves praise in the future and forevermore! We agree! We agree!
